questions.questionText: Let $$f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ccc}-\mathrm{a} & \text { if } & -\mathrm{a} \leq x \leq 0 \\ x+\mathrm{a} & \text { if } & 0< x \leq \mathrm{a}\end{array}\right.$$ where $$\mathrm{a}> 0$$ and $$\mathrm{g}(x)=(f(|x|)-|f(x)|) / 2$$. Then the function $$g:[-a, a] \rightarrow[-a, a]$$ is
options:
(A) neither one-one nor onto.
(B) both one-one and onto.
(C) one-one.
(D) onto
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f: \mathbf{R} \rightarrow \mathbf{R}$ be defined as $f(x)=\frac{2 x^2-3 x+2}{3 x^2+x+3}$. Then $f$ is :
options:
(A)

both one-one and onto

(B)

one-one but not onto

(C)

onto but not one-one

(D)

neither one-one nor onto
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the range of the function $$f(x)=\frac{1}{2+\sin 3 x+\cos 3 x}, x \in \mathbb{R}$$ be $$[a, b]$$. If $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ ar respectively the A.M. and the G.M. of $$a$$ and $$b$$, then $$\frac{\alpha}{\beta}$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$A=\{(x, y): 2 x+3 y=23, x, y \in \mathbb{N}\}$$ and $$B=\{x:(x, y) \in A\}$$. Then the number of one-one functions from $$A$$ to $$B$$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $\mathbb{R}$ denote the set of all real numbers. Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ and $g: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow(0,4)$ be functions defined by

$$ f(x)=\log _e\left(x^2+2 x+4\right), \text { and } g(x)=\frac{4}{1+e^{-2 x}} $$

Define the composite function $f \circ g^{-1}$ by $\left(f \circ g^{-1}\right)(x)=f\left(g^{-1}(x)\right)$, where $g^{-1}$ is the inverse of the function $g$.

Then the value of the derivative of the composite function $f \circ g^{-1}$ at $x=2$ is ________________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the domains of the functions $f(x)=\log _4 \log _3 \log _7\left(8-\log _2\left(x^2+4 x+5\right)\right)$ and $\mathrm{g}(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{7 x+10}{x-2}\right)$ be $(\alpha, \beta)$ and $[\gamma, \delta]$, respectively. Then $\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2+\delta^2$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
For a suitably chosen real constant a, let a

function, $$f:R - \left\{ { - a} \right\} \to R$$ be defined by

$$f(x) = {{a - x} \over {a + x}}$$. Further suppose that for any real number $$x \ne - a$$ and $$f(x) \ne - a$$,

(fof)(x) = x. Then $$f\left( { - {1 \over 2}} \right)$$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Suppose that a function f : R $$ \to $$ R satisfies
f(x + y) = f(x)f(y) for all x, y $$ \in $$ R and f(1) = 3.
If $$\sum\limits_{i = 1}^n {f(i)} = 363$$ then n is equal to ________ .

questions.questionText: Let $f, g:(1, \infty) \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be defined as $f(x)=\frac{2 x+3}{5 x+2}$ and $g(x)=\frac{2-3 x}{1-x}$. If the range of the function fog: $[2,4] \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ is $[\alpha, \beta]$, then $\frac{1}{\beta-\alpha}$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let f : R → R be defined as f (x) = 2x – 1 and g : R - {1} → R be defined as g(x) =
$${{x - {1 \over 2}} \over {x - 1}}$$.
Then the composition function f(g(x)) is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of functions $$f$$, from the set $$\mathrm{A}=\left\{x \in \mathbf{N}: x^{2}-10 x+9 \leq 0\right\}$$ to the set $$\mathrm{B}=\left\{\mathrm{n}^{2}: \mathrm{n} \in \mathbf{N}\right\}$$ such that $$f(x) \leq(x-3)^{2}+1$$, for every $$x \in \mathrm{A}$$, is ___________.

questions.questionText: The range of the function $f(x)=\sqrt{3-x}+\sqrt{2+x}$ is :

questions.questionText: Let $A=\{1,2,3,5,8,9\}$. Then the number of possible functions $f: A \rightarrow A$ such that $f(m \cdot n)=f(m) \cdot f(n)$ for every $m, n \in A$ with $m \cdot n \in A$ is equal to ___________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f$ be a function such that $3 f(x)+2 f\left(\frac{m}{19 x}\right)=5 x, x \neq 0$, where $m=\sum\limits_{i=1}^9(i)^2$. Then $f(5)-f(2)$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If a function $$f$$ satisfies $$f(\mathrm{~m}+\mathrm{n})=f(\mathrm{~m})+f(\mathrm{n})$$ for all $$\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n} \in \mathbf{N}$$ and $$f(1)=1$$, then the largest natural number $$\lambda$$ such that $$\sum_\limits{\mathrm{k}=1}^{2022} f(\lambda+\mathrm{k}) \leq(2022)^2$$ is equal to _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let the sets A and B denote the domain and range respectively of the function $$f(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\lceil x\rceil-x}}$$, where $$\lceil x\rceil$$ denotes the smallest integer greater than or equal to $$x$$. Then among the statements

(S1) : $$A \cap B=(1, \infty)-\mathbb{N}$$ and

(S2) : $$A \cup B=(1, \infty)$$
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let ℕ denote the set of all natural numbers, and ℤ denote the set of all integers. Consider the functions f: ℕ → ℤ and g: ℤ → ℕ defined by

$$ f(n) = \begin{cases} \frac{(n + 1)}{2} & \text{if } n \text{ is odd,} \\ \frac{(4-n)}{2} & \text{if } n \text{ is even,} \end{cases} $$

and

$$ g(n) = \begin{cases} 3 + 2n & \text{if } n \ge 0 , \\ -2n & \text{if } n < 0 . \end{cases} $$

Define $$(g \circ f)(n) = g(f(n))$$ for all $n \in \mathbb{N}$, and $$(f \circ g)(n) = f(g(n))$$ for all $n \in \mathbb{Z}$.

Then which of the following statements is (are) TRUE?
options:
(A)

g $\circ $ f is NOT one-one and g $\circ $ f is NOT onto

(B)

f $\circ $ g is NOT one-one but f $\circ $ g is onto
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let ℝ denote the set of all real numbers. Let f: ℝ → ℝ be a function such that f(x) > 0 for all x ∈ ℝ, and f(x+y) = f(x)f(y) for all x, y ∈ ℝ.

Let the real numbers a₁, a₂, ..., a₅₀ be in an arithmetic progression. If f(a₃₁) = 64f(a₂₅), and

$ \sum\limits_{i=1}^{50} f(a_i) = 3(2^{25}+1), $

then the value of

$ \sum\limits_{i=6}^{30} f(a_i) $

is ________________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The function $f: \mathbf{N}-\{1\} \rightarrow \mathbf{N}$; defined by $f(\mathrm{n})=$ the highest prime factor of $\mathrm{n}$, is :

questions.questionText:
The number of functions f from {1, 2, 3, ...., 20} onto {1, 2, 3, ...., 20} such that f(k) is a multiple of 3,
whenever k is a multiple of 4, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $$\mathrm{D}$$ be the domain of the function $$f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\log _{3 x}\left(\frac{6+2 \log _{3} x}{-5 x}\right)\right)$$. If the range of the function $$\mathrm{g}: \mathrm{D} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$$ defined by $$\mathrm{g}(x)=x-[x],([x]$$ is the greatest integer function), is $$(\alpha, \beta)$$, then $$\alpha^{2}+\frac{5}{\beta}$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $$f:R \to R$$ be a function defined by

$$f(x) = {\left( {2\left( {1 - {{{x^{25}}} \over 2}} \right)(2 + {x^{25}})} \right)^{{1 \over {50}}}}$$. If the function $$g(x) = f(f(f(x))) + f(f(x))$$, then the greatest integer less than or equal to g(1) is ____________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let f : N $$\to$$ R be a function such that $$f(x + y) = 2f(x)f(y)$$ for natural numbers x and y. If f(1) = 2, then the value of $$\alpha$$ for which

$$\sum\limits_{k = 1}^{10} {f(\alpha + k) = {{512} \over 3}({2^{20}} - 1)} $$

holds, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of functions $f:\{1,2,3,4\} \rightarrow\{a, b, c\}$, which are not onto, is :

questions.questionText:

$f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4 x+2[x]}{3}\right)$ is $[\alpha, \beta]$, then $12(\alpha+\beta)$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: Let $$f(x)=\frac{1}{7-\sin 5 x}$$ be a function defined on $$\mathbf{R}$$. Then the range of the function $$f(x)$$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: Let $f$ be a polynomial function such that $\log _2(f(x))=\left(\log _2\left(2+\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{9}+\ldots \ldots \infty\right)\right) \cdot \log _3\left(1+\frac{f(x)}{f(1 / x)}\right), x>0$ and $f(6)=37$. Then $\sum\limits_{\mathrm{n}=1}^{10} f(\mathrm{n})$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let the function $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be defined by

$$ f(x)=\frac{\sin x}{e^{\pi x}} \frac{\left(x^{2023}+2024 x+2025\right)}{\left(x^2-x+3\right)}+\frac{2}{e^{\pi x}} \frac{\left(x^{2023}+2024 x+2025\right)}{\left(x^2-x+3\right)} . $$

Then the number of solutions of $f(x)=0$ in $\mathbb{R}$ is _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$ be a function such that $f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y)$ for all $x, y \in \mathbb{R}$, and $g: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow(0, \infty)$ be a function such that $g(x+y)=g(x) g(y)$ for all $x, y \in \mathbb{R}$. If $f\left(\frac{-3}{5}\right)=12$ and $g\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right)=2$, then the value of $\left(f\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)+g(-2)-8\right) g(0)$ is _________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Consider the function $$f: \mathbb{R} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$$ defined by $$f(x)=\frac{2 x}{\sqrt{1+9 x^2}}$$. If the composition of $$f, \underbrace{(f \circ f \circ f \circ \cdots \circ f)}_{10 \text { times }}(x)=\frac{2^{10} x}{\sqrt{1+9 \alpha x^2}}$$, then the value of $$\sqrt{3 \alpha+1}$$ is equal to _______.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let the function f : [0, 1] $$ \to $$ R be defined by

$$f(x) = {{{4^x}} \over {{4^x} + 2}}$$

Then the value of $$f\left( {{1 \over {40}}} \right) + f\left( {{2 \over {40}}} \right) + f\left( {{3 \over {40}}} \right) + ... + f\left( {{{39} \over {40}}} \right) - f\left( {{1 \over 2}} \right)$$ is ..........
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let the function $$f:(0,\pi ) \to R$$ be defined by $$f(\theta ) = {(\sin \theta + \cos \theta )^2} + {(\sin \theta - \cos \theta )^4}$$

Suppose the function f has a local minimum at $$\theta $$ precisely when $$\theta \in \{ {\lambda _1}\pi ,....,{\lambda _r}\pi \} $$, where $$0 < {\lambda _1} < ...{\lambda _r} < 1$$. Then the value of $${\lambda _1} + ... + {\lambda _r}$$ is .............
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $\mathrm{A}=\{1,2,3,4,5,6\}$. The number of one-one functions $f: \mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{A}$ such that $f(1) \geq 3, f(3) \leq 4$ and $f(2)+f(3)=5$, is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $\mathbb{N}$ denote the set of all positive integers. Consider the sets

$$ A=\{1,2,3,4,5\} \text { and } B=\{1,2,3,4,5,6,7\} . $$

Let $S$ be the set of all functions $f: A \rightarrow B$ such that $f(2) \neq 2$ and $f(4) \neq 4$. Consider the set $T=\left\{f \in S:\right.$ there exists a function $g: B \rightarrow \mathbb{N}$ such that $g(f(x))=2^x$ for all $\left.x \in A\right\}$.

Then the number of elements in the set $T$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let $$|M|$$ denote the determinant of a square matrix $$M$$. Let $$g:\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right] \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$$ be the function defined by

$$ g(\theta)=\sqrt{f(\theta)-1}+\sqrt{f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\theta\right)-1} $$

where

$$ f(\theta)=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & \sin \theta & 1 \\ -\sin \theta & 1 & \sin \theta \\ -1 & -\sin \theta & 1 \end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \sin \pi & \cos \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \tan \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ \sin \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & -\cos \frac{\pi}{2} & \log _{e}\left(\frac{4}{\pi}\right) \\ \cot \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \log _{e}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \tan \pi \end{array}\right| . $$

Let $$p(x)$$ be a quadratic polynomial whose roots are the maximum and minimum values of the function $$g(\theta)$$, and $$p(2)=2-\sqrt{2}$$. Then, which of the following is/are TRUE ?
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If the domain of the function

$f(x) = \sqrt{\log_{(0.6)} (\left| \frac{2x-5}{x^2-4} \right|)}$ is $(-\infty, a] \cup \{b\} \cup [c, d) \cup (e, \infty)$, then the value of $a + b + c + d + e$ is ________.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let [ • ] denote the greatest integer function. If the domain of the function $f(x)=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+[x]}{3}\right)$ is $[\alpha, \beta)$, then $\alpha^2+\beta^2$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $$f(x)=\frac{4 x+3}{6 x-4}, x \neq \frac{2}{3}$$ and $$(f \circ f)(x)=g(x)$$, where $$g: \mathbb{R}-\left\{\frac{2}{3}\right\} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}-\left\{\frac{2}{3}\right\}$$, then $$(g ogog)(4)$$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f:[0,1] \rightarrow[0,1]$ be the function defined by $f(x)=\frac{x^3}{3}-x^2+\frac{5}{9} x+\frac{17}{36}$. Consider the square region $S=[0,1] \times[0,1]$. Let $G=\{(x, y) \in S: y>f(x)\}$ be called the green region and $R=\{(x, y) \in S: y < f(x)\}$ be called the red region. Let $L_h=\{(x, h) \in S: x \in[0,1]\}$ be the horizontal line drawn at a height $h \in[0,1]$. Then which of the following statements is(are) true?
options: (A) There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the green region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the green region below the line $L_h$
(B) There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the red region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the red region below the line $L_h$
(C) There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the green region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the red region below the line $L_h$
(D) There exists an $h \in\left[\frac{1}{4}, \frac{2}{3}\right]$ such that the area of the red region above the line $L_h$ equals the area of the green region below the line $L_k$
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $S=(0,1) \cup(1,2) \cup(3,4)$ and $T=\{0,1,2,3\}$. Then which of the following statements is(are) true?
options: (A) There are infinitely many functions from $S$ to $T$
(B) There are infinitely many strictly increasing functions from $S$ to $T$
(C) The number of continuous functions from $S$ to $T$ is at most 120
(D) Every continuous function from $S$ to $T$ is differentiable
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the domain of the function $f(x)=\log _7\left(1-\log _4\left(x^2-9 x+18\right)\right)$ is $(\alpha, \beta) \cup(\gamma, o)$, then $\alpha+\beta+\gamma+\hat{o}$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f$ be a function such that $f(x)+3 f\left(\frac{24}{x}\right)=4 x, x \neq 0$. Then $f(3)+f(8)$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f:[0,3] \rightarrow$ A be defined by $f(x)=2 x^3-15 x^2+36 x+7$ and $g:[0, \infty) \rightarrow B$ be defined by $g(x)=\frac{x^{2025}}{x^{2025}+1}$, If both the functions are onto and $S=\{ x \in Z ; x \in A$ or $x \in B \}$, then $n(S)$ is equal to :

questions.questionText: If f(x) and g(x) are two polynomials such that the polynomial P(x) = f(x<sup>3</sup>) + x g(x<sup>3</sup>) is divisible by x<sup>2</sup> + x + 1, then P(1) is equal to ___________.

questions.questionText: If the domain of the function $ \log_5(18x - x^2 - 77) $ is $ (\alpha, \beta) $ and the domain of the function $ \log_{(x-1)} \left( \frac{2x^2 + 3x - 2}{x^2 - 3x - 4} \right) $ is $(\gamma, \delta)$, then $ \alpha^2 + \beta^2 + \gamma^2 $ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the domain of the function $f(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{10+3 x-x^2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{x+|x|}}$ is $(a, b)$, then $(1+a)^2+b^2$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The domain of the function $$f(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{[x]^{2}-3[x]-10}}$$ is : ( where $$[\mathrm{x}]$$ denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to $$x$$ )
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
The sum of all the elements in the range of $f(x) = \text{Sgn}(\sin x) + \text{Sgn}(\cos x) + \text{Sgn}(\tan x) + \text{Sgn}(\cot x)$, $x \neq \frac{n\pi}{2}, n \in \mathbb{Z}$, where

$\text{Sgn}(t) = \begin{cases} 1, & \text{if } t > 0 \\ -1, & \text{if } t < 0 \end{cases}$

is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : The function $f: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ defined by $f(x) = \frac{x}{1 + |x|}$ is one-one.

Statement II : The function $f: \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ defined by $f(x) = \frac{x^2 + 4x - 30}{x^2 - 8x + 18}$ is many-one.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A)

Statement I is true but Statement II is false

(D)

Statement I is false but Statement II is true
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $\mathrm{A}=\{1,2,3,4\}$ and $\mathrm{B}=\{1,4,9,16\}$. Then the number of many-one functions $f: \mathrm{A} \rightarrow \mathrm{B}$ such that $1 \in f(\mathrm{~A})$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the range of $$f(\theta)=\frac{\sin ^4 \theta+3 \cos ^2 \theta}{\sin ^4 \theta+\cos ^2 \theta}, \theta \in \mathbb{R}$$ is $$[\alpha, \beta]$$, then the sum of the infinite G.P., whose first term is 64 and the common ratio is $$\frac{\alpha}{\beta}$$, is equal to __________.
questions.subject: Mathematics
